skill.name: python-fastapi-development
skill.description: Guides production FastAPI backend development with async project setup, SQLAlchemy, Pydantic, authentication, testing, documentation, and deployment workflows.
skill.summary: Build production FastAPI backends with structured workflow guidance from setup through deployment.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are false positives in Markdown workflow text. Backticks are code fences or inline skill names, and weak-crypto matches come from words such as description and design. No prompt injection, malware intent, credential access, network exfiltration, or executable payload was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as External Commands
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown inline code or copy-paste prompt fences. They reference other skills and workflow prompts, but they do not execute shell commands, run scripts, or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown bullets or fenced prompt examples. No command runner, shell syntax, script file, or executable code path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography findings match ordinary documentation words such as description, design, and database-design. The skill does not implement DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence lines contain workflow prose and skill names, not crypto APIs or algorithm implementations. No cryptographic code exists in the scanned Markdown file.

content.value_statement: FastAPI projects need consistent structure, validation, testing, and deployment decisions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a staged workflow for building Python API backends.
actual_capabilities: Guides FastAPI project setup with Python environment, app structure, logging, and configuration steps.
Covers database planning with PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy models, Pydantic models, migrations, and sessions.
Organizes API route work for endpoint design, CRUD operations, validation, and response models.
Includes authentication planning for JWT, OAuth2, registration, login endpoints, middleware, and password hashing.
Adds workflow phases for error handling, testing, documentation, deployment, and quality gates.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not a full FastAPI code generator by itself.
It references companion skills that may not be installed in every environment.
It does not include ready-made templates, scripts, or source files beyond SKILL.md.
Deployment advice remains general and must be adapted to the target cloud or hosting platform.

use_cases.title: Start a New API Service
use_cases.description: Create a clear implementation plan for a new FastAPI service with database, routes, tests, and deployment steps.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team API Workflows
use_cases.description: Use one staged checklist for setup, authentication, documentation, and quality gates across Python backend projects.

use_cases.title: Review a FastAPI Build Plan
use_cases.description: Check whether a planned service includes validation, error handling, tests, OpenAPI documentation, and production configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic FastAPI Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this FastAPI workflow to plan a new Python API project. Include project structure, environment setup, logging, and configuration tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Design Models and Routes
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this workflow to design a FastAPI service with PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy models, Pydantic schemas, CRUD routes, and validation.

prompt_templates.title: Add Authentication and Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to extend my FastAPI backend with JWT authentication, password hashing, pytest coverage, and integration tests.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Production Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FastAPI backend against this workflow. Identify gaps in error handling, OpenAPI documentation, Docker setup, security checks, and quality gates.

output_examples.input: Plan a new FastAPI backend for a task management product.
output: A staged project plan covering setup, database schema, routes, authentication, tests, documentation, and deployment.
A checklist of quality gates such as type checking, linting, security scan, and test coverage.

output_examples.input: Review my existing FastAPI service before launch.
output: A gap analysis grouped by workflow phase.
Specific next actions for missing validation, error handling, OpenAPI documentation, or production configuration.

output_examples.input: Help me organize FastAPI authentication work.
output: A practical sequence for choosing JWT or OAuth2, creating registration and login endpoints, and adding middleware.
Testing and security checks to run before merging the authentication changes.

best_practices: Start with the workflow phase that matches your current project state.
Pair the checklist with concrete repository context before asking for implementation details.
Validate generated plans with tests, type checks, linting, and security review before release.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the workflow as a replacement for reviewing generated code.
Do not skip database and authentication design before creating routes.
Do not deploy without adapting Docker, environment, and security settings to your platform.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete FastAPI application?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured workflow that guides planning and implementation across common FastAPI backend phases.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Which database stack does it target?
faq.answer: It focuses on PostgreSQL with SQLAlchemy, Alembic migrations, session management, and Pydantic models.

faq.question: Does it include authentication guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers choosing JWT or OAuth2, registration, login endpoints, middleware, and password hashing.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: The static findings were false positives from Markdown syntax and ordinary words. No executable payload or malicious intent was found.

faq.question: What should I provide when using this skill?
faq.answer: Provide your project goals, current repository state, database needs, authentication requirements, and deployment target.
